column1: === DiskANN vs Divergence: Cohere 100K, dim=768, k=100, single-thread ===
Hardware: EC2 i4i (NVMe SSD)
Dataset: Cohere 100K, 768d, L2-normalized, cosine metric
Ground truth: brute-force top-100 (same GT file, SHA256 verified)
DiskANN: Rust v0.48.0, Vamana disk-index, R=32, L_build=200, quantization_type=FP
NOTE: disk-index pipeline generates PQ auxiliary files (pq_compressed.bin 9.2MB,
pq_pivots.bin 776KB) loaded into DRAM for candidate pre-filtering.
Divergence: NSW, m_max=32, ef_c=200, v3 BFS page-packed adjacency
=== DiskANN Search Results ===
Build: R=32, L=200, 1 thread, 151.9s
Search: beam_width=4, 1 thread
Source: diskann-benchmark (Rust)
--- num_nodes_to_cache=0 (no cache) ---
L   recall    QPS   mean_lat_us   p95_us   p999_us   IOs/q   cache_hit%
--- num_nodes_to_cache=250 (~1MB) ---
L   recall    QPS   mean_lat_us   IOs/q   cache_hit%
--- num_nodes_to_cache=1000 (~4MB) ---
L   recall    QPS   mean_lat_us   IOs/q   cache_hit%
--- num_nodes_to_cache=5000 (~20MB) ---
L   recall    QPS   mean_lat_us   IOs/q   cache_hit%
DiskANN cache observation: cache_hit% = 0.0% across ALL cache sizes.
IOs/q unchanged at every L regardless of cache. Caching has no measurable
effect. This may be because at 100K scale the medoid/entry node set is trivial,
or because the DiskANN Rust cache implementation doesn't cache the sectors
being read during search (only caches BFS-ordered warm nodes at startup).
=== Divergence v3 Search Results ===
Build: m_max=32, ef_c=200, v3 BFS reorder, 1 thread
--- Warm (5% cache, cross-query warming) ---
config           ef   recall    QPS   p50_ms   p99_ms   phy/q    hit%
--- Per-query cold (pool.clear() before each query, no cross-query state) ---
config           ef   recall    QPS   p50_ms   p99_ms   phy/q    hit%
=== Iso-Recall Comparison: recall ≈ 96.8% ===
Latency metric: DiskANN reports mean, Divergence reports p50 (median).
QPS = nq / wall_seconds — comparable across both (unambiguous).
recall   QPS    lat_ms*   IO/q    DRAM_est   cache_config
DiskANN L=500 no$      96.78   47.4    21.1m    506.1    ~10 MB    no cache
Divg v3-perq ef225     96.8   102.5     8.7p    148.5    ~295 MB   1MB pool, perq clear
* m = mean latency, p = p50 (median). Not directly comparable.
$ = baseline (no cache)
At iso-recall 96.8%:
QPS:  Divergence v3-perq-cold (102.5) vs DiskANN no-cache (47.4) = 2.16x
QPS:  Divergence v3-warm (125.8) vs DiskANN c5000 (50.5) = 2.49x
IO/q: Divergence v3-perq (148.5) vs DiskANN (506.1) = 3.41x fewer IOs
IO/q: Divergence v3-warm (126.2) vs DiskANN (506.1) = 4.01x fewer IOs
=== Iso-Recall Comparison: recall ≈ 97.3% ===
recall   QPS    lat_ms*   IO/q
QPS:  Divergence v3-perq (92.8) vs DiskANN (40.9) = 2.27x
IO/q: Divergence v3-perq (161.2) vs DiskANN (605.7) = 3.76x fewer IOs
NOTE: Recall not exactly aligned (97.3 vs 97.8). DiskANN is 0.5pp higher.
The comparison slightly favors Divergence (higher recall costs more IO).
=== Analysis ===
1. QPS ADVANTAGE IS CONSISTENT ACROSS RECALL LEVELS:
At recall≈96.8%: Divergence 2.2-2.5x faster (perq-cold to warm range)
At recall≈97.3%: Divergence 2.3x faster (perq-cold)
The gap is structural, not a single-point artifact.
2. IO REDUCTION IS THE PRIMARY DRIVER:
DiskANN IOs/q ≈ L (each hop = 1 sector read).
Divergence phy/q = 148.5 at ef=225 perq-cold (226 expansions → 148 reads).
The gap comes from:
a) BFS page packing: co-accessed VIDs share pages → fewer unique page reads
b) Vectors in DRAM: Divergence scores from DRAM, zero IO for vector access.
DiskANN co-locates vectors in sectors, so every hop reads vector data too.
3. DiskANN GRAPH QUALITY GAP:
At L=200 (same search budget as ef=200):
DiskANN: 83.7% recall
Divergence: 96.3% recall
DiskANN needs L=500 (2.5x more hops) to match Divergence ef=200.
This gap likely comes from graph construction differences:
- Vamana alpha-pruning may be too aggressive for cosine/high-dim
- DiskANN disk-index build uses PQ-compressed distances during construction
- NSW bidirectional edge insertion may produce better local connectivity
Needs DiskANN R=64 or L_build=300 test to confirm.
4. DiskANN CACHE IS INEFFECTIVE AT 100K:
Cache hit% = 0.0% at all cache sizes (250/1000/5000 nodes).
IOs/q unchanged. QPS improves marginally (47.4 → 50.5 at L=500 with 5000 nodes).
The ~6% QPS gain may be from reduced PQ preprocessing (cached nodes skip PQ),
not from IO reduction.
5. MEMORY TRADEOFF — THE ELEPHANT IN THE ROOM:
Divergence DRAM: ~295 MB (293 MB vectors + 782 KB adj_index + cache)
DiskANN DRAM: ~10 MB (9.2 MB PQ + 776 KB pivots)
Divergence uses 30x more DRAM. This is the structural reason it does fewer IOs.
At Cohere 100K (295 MB), this is trivial.
At Cohere 1B, Divergence would need ~2.9 TB DRAM for vectors — impractical.
DiskANN's architecture is designed for the regime where vectors don't fit in DRAM.
Fair headline: "At equal dataset size where vectors fit in DRAM, Divergence is
2.2x faster and does 3.4x fewer IOs. The advantage comes from DRAM vectors +
page packing, at the cost of 30x more memory."
=== Disk Usage (measured) ===
Component                    DiskANN               Divergence v3
disk.index                   391 MB                -
pq_compressed.bin             9.2 MB               -
pq_pivots.bin                776 KB                -
adjacency_pages.dat          -                     6.1 MB (1537 pages × 4KB)
adj_index.dat                -                     782 KB
vectors.dat                  -                     293 MB
Total on disk                402 MB                300 MB
=== DRAM Usage (measured) ===
Component                    DiskANN               Divergence v3
Vectors                      0 (on disk)           293 MB (FP32, VectorBank)
PQ codes                     9.2 MB                0
PQ pivots                    776 KB                0
adj_index                    N/A                   782 KB
AdjacencyPool cache          N/A                   32KB-4MB (configurable)
Total DRAM                   ~10 MB                ~295 MB
=== Remaining TODO ===
1. [ ] DiskANN with R=64, L_build=300: test if graph quality gap narrows
2. [ ] OS-level /proc/diskstats validation for both systems
3. [ ] Report p50 for DiskANN (need to parse per-query latency distribution)
4. [ ] Multi-thread comparison (both systems)
